title: sisymbrella aspera subsp. pseudoboissieri
intro: Sisymbrella aspera subsp. pseudoboissieri, also known as the Australian daisy bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Sisymbrella aspera subsp. pseudoboissieri can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
